# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 91

This is turn 91. Turns 1 through 90 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 91.

## Turn 91 — coprime kernels eliminate the terminal generation condition

The candidate remains unchanged:

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0

x0^2*x1^4*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The tame relation is implicit. Mathematically, the exponent is the genuine profinite idempotent \(\omega _2\); \(40491355905\) is its required residue modulo \(85667662080\). 

Turn 90 proved that a fixed-frame target whose wild chief factors are all scalar has a canonical decomposition

\[
G\cong H\times_{H_2}Q,
\]

where \(H=G/L\) is tame, \(H_2\) is its maximal \(2\)-quotient, \(Q\) is a finite \(2\)-group, and the two kernels

\[
N=\ker(H\to H_2),\qquad L=\ker(Q\to H_2)
\]

have respectively odd and \(2\)-power order. 

Turn 90 retained the literal tuple-generation condition rather than asserting that surjective projections generate the pullback. Here the coprime kernels make that assertion valid.

### Coprime-kernel subdirect lemma

Let

\[
A\overset{\pi_A}{\longrightarrow}C,\qquad
B\overset{\pi_B}{\longrightarrow}C
\]

be surjections of finite groups, with kernels \(U,V\) of coprime orders. If

\[
J\le A\times_C B
\]

projects surjectively onto both \(A\) and \(B\), then

\[
J=A\times_C B.
\]

Indeed,

\[
|J|=|A|\,|J\cap V|
      =|B|\,|J\cap U|.
\]

Using \(|A|=|C||U|\) and \(|B|=|C||V|\), we obtain

\[
[U:J\cap U]=[V:J\cap V].
\]

The common value divides both \(|U|\) and \(|V|\), so coprimality forces it to be \(1\). Thus \(J\) contains both kernels and has full order.

Applying this to \(A=H\), \(B=Q\), \(U=N\), and \(V=L\) gives:

\[
J\le H\times_{H_2}Q,\quad
\operatorname{pr}_H(J)=H,\quad
\operatorname{pr}_Q(J)=Q
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
J=G.
\tag{91.1}
\]

### Exact fixed-frame epimorphism bijection

Fix a tame epimorphism

\[
\alpha:T_{\mathrm{tame}}\twoheadrightarrow H
\]

and let

\[
\alpha_2:\Pi\twoheadrightarrow H_2
\]

be its induced map on the common marked maximal pro-\(2\) quotient

\[
\Pi=
\left\langle \sigma,x_0,x_1\ \middle|\
x_0^2x_1^3x_1^\sigma=1
\right\rangle_{\mathrm{pro}\text{-}2}.
\]

Let \(q:Q\to H_2\). For either source

\[
\Gamma\in\{\Gamma_A,G_{\mathbb Q_2}\},
\]

a homomorphism \(f:\Gamma\to G\) inducing \(\alpha\) is uniquely equivalent, by the universal property of the pullback, to a homomorphism

\[
\beta:\Pi\to Q
\]

satisfying

\[
q\beta=\alpha_2.
\tag{91.2}
\]

Moreover,

\[
f\text{ is surjective}
\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
\beta\text{ is surjective}.
\tag{91.3}
\]

The forward implication is immediate. Conversely, if \(\beta\) is surjective, the image of \(f\) projects onto both \(H\) and \(Q\), so (91.1) makes it the full pullback.

Thus Turn 90’s generation condition can be removed entirely. If \(\theta:G\to E\) is a scalar decoration, it kills the odd subgroup \(N\) and factors as \(\bar\theta:Q\to E\). Consequently there is an exact bijection

\[
\operatorname{Sur}_{\alpha,\theta=\psi}(\Gamma,G)
\cong
\left\{
\beta\in\operatorname{Sur}(\Pi,Q):
q\beta=\alpha_2,\ 
\bar\theta\beta=\psi
\right\}.
\tag{91.4}
\]

The right-hand set is literally the same for \(\Gamma_A\) and \(G_{\mathbb Q_2}\), because their generator-marked maximal pro-\(2\) quotients are both \(\Pi\). Hence equality in the terminal scalar branch is a bijection of epimorphism sets, not merely equality obtained after counting.

### Converse: an exact classification of the terminal branch

The pullback condition also implies that every \(G\)-chief factor in \(L\) is trivial.

Indeed, \(N\) centralizes \(L\), while

\[
G/N\cong Q
\]

is a \(2\)-group. Therefore the action on every elementary abelian chief factor inside \(L\) factors through a \(2\)-group. In characteristic \(2\), every simple module for a finite \(2\)-group is trivial.

Thus:

\[
\boxed{
\begin{aligned}
&\text{Every wild chief factor in }L\text{ is scalar}\\
&\qquad\Longleftrightarrow\\
&G\cong H\times_{H_2}Q
\text{ for a finite }2\text{-group }Q.
\end{aligned}}
\tag{91.5}
\]

The odd subgroup in Turn 90 is consequently \(O_{2'}(G)\), and the pullback description is intrinsic.

This turns the terminal branch of the fixed-frame induction into a complete structural classification with an exact pro-\(2\) epimorphism formula. There is no remaining Goursat or generation ambiguity in that branch.

**ESTABLISHED**: Strengthened the Turn-90 terminal scalar theorem. Proved that, because the two pullback kernels have coprime orders, a map to \(H\times_{H_2}Q\) is surjective exactly when its \(H\)- and \(Q\)-projections are surjective. Therefore fixed-frame decorated epimorphisms are in exact bijection with compatible epimorphisms from the common marked maximal pro-\(2\) quotient to \(Q\). Also proved the converse, giving an iff classification of terminal scalar targets as these canonical pullbacks.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun. The nonterminal proof still relies on the ordinary Thompson critical-subgroup input and the finite-Heisenberg and Wall–Arf/local-Kummer propositions from Turn 89.

**NEXT**: Replace Turn 90’s literal generation clause by the bijection (91.4), insert the iff classification (91.5) as the complete induction base, and submit the unchanged residue block to the official verifier.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
